verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Afternoon — 16:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: For TODAY, I’d keep it a cam-check situation for Rey Cortiço in the afternoon around 16:00. Surfline looks poor-to-fair and the wind angle isn’t great for that spot, so it could be a bit hit-and-miss even though the tide is rising and might help the sandbanks get punchier. Before I leave, I’ll check the Surfline cams (and MEO Beachcam since that’s my usual backup) and only go if it looks clearly better than expected.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 26 kJ, 6 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 24 kJ, 5 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
